commands.repair_reason: No automated repair is indicated; the remaining action is maintainer review of the shared-token approval-runtime trust boundary and normal required checks.; Needs attention: No supply-chain change is present, but the diff intentionally changes an internal approval-runtime authentication boundary that needs maintainer acceptance before merge. commands.review_summary:
This PR makes the gateway approval-runtime token helper prefer an existing `exec-approvals.json` socket token, keeps the process-local fallback, and adds focused tests for shared-token and remote/explicit URL omission behavior.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +105. Total +127 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main only exposes a process-local approval runtime token, so a separate approval-runtime process sharing only `exec-approvals.json` cannot know that token; I verified this from the current source path rather than running a failing smoke.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is indicated; the remaining action is maintainer review of the shared-token approval-runtime trust boundary and normal required checks.

target.title: fix(gateway): share approval runtime socket token
target.body:
## Summary
- Use the existing `exec-approvals.json` socket token as the approval-runtime token when it exists, so Docker/CLI/separate-process approval clients sharing `OPENCLAW_HOME` can authenticate as the local approval runtime.
- Keep the process-local random token as the fallback when no shared socket token exists, without creating `exec-approvals.json` just to read a token.
- Keep `approvalRuntimeToken` attached only for generated local gateway URL sources; env, explicit, and configured remote URL sources omit it even when the host is loopback.

## Scope split
- Split out from #86771.
- This PR is only shared approval-runtime token selection for Docker/CLI/separate-process approval clients sharing OpenClaw state.
- It does not include stale Discord button UX or the `approval.routeNotice.send` gateway method.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: A separate approval-runtime process sharing `OPENCLAW_HOME` can authenticate with the existing `exec-approvals.json` socket token and resolve a requester-bound approval. Env/configured remote gateway URL sources still omit `approvalRuntimeToken` and cannot resolve requester-bound approvals without normal owner visibility/runtime authority.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on PR head `a395cede70`, Node `v24.14.0`, macOS host. The smoke used an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`, a real `exec-approvals.json` socket token, a real loopback gateway server, a parent Node process, a separate child Node process, and real gateway clients.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a `pnpm exec tsx` smoke from the repo root. The smoke wrote a temporary `.openclaw/exec-approvals.json`, started a real gateway, created requester-bound approvals, ran a child Node process sharing `OPENCLAW_HOME` to resolve one approval through `withOperatorApprovalsGatewayClient`, then checked that env override and configured remote loopback clients could connect but could not resolve requester-bound approvals.

Evidence after fix:

```text
$ PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm exec tsx <shared-runtime-current-head-smoke>
[gateway] starting HTTP server...
[gateway] http server listening (0 plugins)
[gateway] ready
SMOKE shared_runtime_current_head ok {"head":"a395cede70","gatewayUrl":"ws://127.0.0.1:<port>","sharedTokenFile":"<OPENCLAW_HOME>/.openclaw/exec-approvals.json","childProcess":"CHILD_SHARED_RUNTIME_RESOLVE ok","envOverrideRejected":true,"remoteLoopbackRejected":true,"sharedResolve":"allow-once"}
[shutdown] completed cleanly in 14ms
```

Observed result after fix: The second process resolved the requester-bound approval using the shared socket token. Env override and configured remote loopback clients could connect to the gateway, but both were rejected from resolving requester-bound approvals because those URL sources do not receive `approvalRuntimeToken`.

What was not tested: No Docker Compose smoke was run. The live proof used two Node processes on one host sharing `OPENCLAW_HOME`. Explicit `gatewayUrl` override omission is covered by focused client-option tests because this helper path has no explicit auth input and cannot authenticate to a token-auth gateway as a live remote client.

## Tests and validation
- `PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/operator-approval-runtime-token.ts src/gateway/operator-approval-runtime-token.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.e2e.test.ts` — passed.
- `PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/operator-approval-runtime-token.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot` — 5 files, 29 tests passed.
- `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` — passed.

## Risk checklist
Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`

Highest-risk area: approval-runtime authentication for local approval clients sharing OpenClaw state.

Mitigation: Shared-token selection only reads an existing socket token; it does not create the shared file. The approval client attaches the runtime token only for generated local gateway URL sources; env, explicit, and configured remote URL sources omit it.
